nodes.label: 1000 True Fans Theory
nodes.description: Kevin Kelly's 2008 theory that creators need only 1,000 true fans paying ~$100/year to make a living, rather than millions of casual followers

nodes.label: Creator Middleman Problem
nodes.description: Centralized social platforms became new intermediaries between creators and fans, inserting ads and algorithmic recommendations while capturing most revenue

nodes.label: NFT Definition
nodes.description: Non-Fungible Token - a blockchain-based record representing unique digital media with verifiable history, provenance, and programmable capabilities

nodes.label: Rent-Seeking Removal
nodes.description: Blockchain-based ownership eliminates rent-seeking intermediaries by giving users full control of purchased NFTs, limiting platform fees through competition

nodes.label: Fine-Grained Price Tiering
nodes.description: NFTs enable creators to capture more consumer surplus through granular pricing from $100K+ items for superfans to $1 items for casual supporters

nodes.label: Users as Owners
nodes.description: Crypto networks turn users into owners through tokens, creating genuine P2P marketing and reducing customer acquisition costs to near zero

nodes.label: Subscribe to Mint
nodes.description: Mirror.xyz product requiring email subscription to mint NFTs, bootstrapping direct creator-collector communication channels for long-term engagement

nodes.label: Genesis Drop
nodes.description: NFT collectible featuring project logo/mascot at launch, adding credibility and catalyzing community formation around project genesis

nodes.label: Collectible Series
nodes.description: Recurring NFT drops providing consistent incentives for new follower subscriptions and brand asset collection completion by existing holders

nodes.label: Writing NFTs
nodes.description: Mirror's approach to tokenizing written content, allowing bootstrapped audiences to collect posts with creators capturing 100% of revenue

nodes.label: Web Evolution
nodes.description: Three phases: Web1 (static sites), Web2 (social/mobile/cloud), Web3 (crypto/AI/decentralization) representing shift from read to read-write to read-write-own

nodes.label: Platform Ownership via Tokens
nodes.description: Web3 enables users to own platforms through tokens, shifting power from centralized corporations to distributed communities

nodes.label: Creator DAO
nodes.description: Decentralized Autonomous Organizations for creators using smart contracts for incorporation, funding, governance, and immediate token liquidity

nodes.label: Curator Economy
nodes.description: Future where curators act like angel investors, building portfolios of NFTs/tokens and proving discovery of creators through transparent on-chain records

nodes.label: Skeuomorphism vs Crypto-Native
nodes.description: Tension between familiar designs mimicking physical objects (YouTube NFTs, Tweet NFTs) versus designs leveraging blockchain's unique properties

nodes.label: NFT Royalty Challenge
nodes.description: 2023 marketplace wars collapsed automatic royalties - OpenSea made royalties optional, Blur enforced 0.5% minimum, revealing gap between code and market reality

nodes.label: NFT Hype Cycle
nodes.description: NFTs followed Gartner Hype Cycle reaching Peak of Inflated Expectations in 2021 ($29B art market), then 93% crash by 2024, with ongoing evolution to practical use cases

edges.description: Kelly's vision was derailed by centralized platforms becoming new middlemen
NFTs provide technology to return to direct creator-fan relationships
Blockchain-based ownership limits platform power to extract fees
NFTs allow fine-grained pricing from dollars to hundreds of thousands
NFT ownership creates sense of investment and community participation
Removing platform fees makes direct fan support economically viable
Variable pricing captures different levels of fan enthusiasm
Subscribe to Mint turns collectors into engaged community members
Subscribe to Mint is ideal mechanism for genesis drops
Email list enables ongoing series drops to engaged audience
Bootstrapped audience can collect Writing NFTs from Mirror posts
Web2 phase brought social platforms that became middlemen
Web3 phase enables user ownership through tokens
Token-based ownership allows DAO governance models
Transparent ownership enables curator investment model
DAOs turn community members into literal co-owners
Choice between familiar metaphors vs blockchain-native experiences
Promised automatic royalties failed under market competition
NFT adoption followed typical technology hype and crash pattern
